secondary_archetype: 0-to-1 / New Bets PM in healthtech / payer-provider

level_signals: 5+ years of product management experience required
Shapes product strategy and defines roadmap
Drives enterprise partnerships with payers and health systems
Title is 'Product Lead' suggesting senior individual contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience with expertise in B2B healthcare products, ideally in revenue cycle management, health IT, or adjacent domains.
Commercial fluency. You've worked closely with sales, partnerships, or BD on early-stage deals
Comfort with ambiguity. You're good at running discovery, forming hypotheses, and updating quickly when you're wrong.
Strong cross-functional credibility. You can talk to ML scientists about model outputs, to lawyers about data use agreements, and to a pharma VP about their clinical development pipeline.
Clear, direct communication. You write and speak well. You can take something complicated and make it simple without losing precision.
Ship 0→1 products that deliver measurable impact. Define and execute on MVP scope that proves value with design partners.

Drive enterprise partnerships with both providers and payers. Partner closely with Abridge's commercial leadership and the clinical and revenue cycle leadership at payers and health systems to understand pain points + operational impact, and validate product direction.

Leverage domain expertise in healthcare data and/or revenue cycle management, e.g. the end-to-end claims lifecycle, payer policies, prior authorization requirements, and how clinical documentation connects to reimbursement.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have built products at the intersection of clinical workflows and financial/administrative operations.
You have direct experience shipping revenue cycle, billing, coding, or prior authorization products.
You understand payer policies, medical necessity criteria, and prior authorization workflows.
You have experience with Epic, Cerner, or other EHR billing and revenue cycle modules.
You have managed design partner relationships or early-stage enterprise pilots in healthcare.
You have experience building collaborative solutions that create value for multiple stakeholders with competing interests.

scope.surface: 0→1 payer-provider bridge products leveraging Abridge's structured clinical data from patient-clinician conversations
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS, health systems and payers
cross_functional_partners: Revenue cycle leaders at health systems
core_responsibilities: Define product strategy and roadmap for payer-provider bridge products
Navigate complex clinical and operational workflows, shifting administrative work left to point of care
Leverage domain expertise in healthcare data and revenue cycle management
Drive enterprise partnerships with providers and payers
Ship 0→1 products that deliver measurable impact and prove value with design partners
Establish clear KPIs around administrative efficiency, time savings, reimbursement cycle time, and user experience
Build cross-functional alignment on roadmap and success metrics

compensation.equity_form: Equity grants for full time employees

green_flags: Clear product mission and problem statement: bridging payer-provider divide using structured clinical data
Equity grants mentioned alongside competitive compensation
Strong benefits package (parental leave, sabbatical, HSA, mental health support)
Defined ownership scope from day-1 on a new product bet
Company has existing payer ecosystem collaborations as announced partnerships — not purely greenfield
red_flags: Office location and remote/hybrid policy not specified in the JD body — unclear whether candidates must be in SF, NYC, or Pittsburgh
0→1 / 'new bets' framing means high ambiguity and potential for pivot; success metrics not yet established
Posting date not available — unknown how long the role has been open

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Product Lead', 5+ years requirement, and 0→1 ownership scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and enterprise partnership focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities section, not explicitly listed as KPIs)
location.type (set to 'unclear' — JD mentions three offices but does not state whether role is remote, hybrid, or onsite)
ambiguities: Location policy not specified: Abridge lists offices in San Francisco, New York, and Pittsburgh, but the JD does not state whether this role requires on-site presence, is hybrid, or is fully remote.

The word 'ideally' in '5+ years of product management experience with expertise in B2B healthcare products, ideally in revenue cycle management, health IT, or adjacent domains' softens the domain specificity, but the overall requirement of 5+ years in healthcare B2B PM is treated as hard given unconditional bullet placement.
